content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

Your task is to find any three indices i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k such that:


1≤i,j,k≤N1 \le i, j, k \le N1≤i,j,k≤N
i≠j,i≠k,j≠ki \ne j, i \ne k, j \ne ki=j,i=k,j=k

That is, the indices {i,j,k}\{i, j, k\}{i,j,k} are pairwise distinct.
Ai+Aj≠AkA_i + A_j \ne A_kAi​+Aj​=Ak​
If there are multiple possible solutions, you may find any of them.

If no solution exists, print −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case,



If no solution exists, output the single integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise, output three distinct space-separated integers i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k denoting the indices you found.

If there are multiple solutions, any of them will be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: With (i,j,k)=(4,2,1)(i, j, k) = (4, 2, 1)(i,j,k)=(4,2,1), we have A4=0,A2=1,A1=2A_4 = 0, A_2 = 1, A_1 = 2A4​=0,A2​=1,A1​=2.
Because 0+1≠20+1\ne 20+1=2, this is a valid triplet.
There are many several other valid solutions, which will all be accepted as well.
Test case 222: It can be verified that no valid triplet of indices exists.
Test case 333: Consider (i,j,k)=(2,3,1)(i, j, k) = (2, 3, 1)(i,j,k)=(2,3,1), which gives Ai+Aj=A2+A3=2A_i + A_j = A_2 + A_3 = 2Ai​+Aj​=A2​+A3​=2 while Ak=A1=4A_k = A_1 = 4Ak​=A1​=4.
Because 2≠42\ne 42=4 this is a valid triplet.
